sections.heading: STAR Framework
sections.content:
• Situation — Context and background
  • Task — What was your responsibility
  • Action — What YOU specifically did
  • Result — Measurable outcome

Keep answers to 1-2 minutes. Be specific, not generic.

sections.heading: Stories You Need Ready
sections.content:
• Performance problem you solved (16 min → 2 sec)
  • Production incident handling
  • Modernizing legacy code
  • Technical disagreement with a colleague
  • A mistake you made and learned from
  • Decision with incomplete information
  • Mentoring a junior developer
  • Delivering under pressure
  • Handling ambiguous requirements

sections.heading: Key Principles
sections.content:
• Be honest — don't claim experience you don't have
  • Show growth — what you learned matters more than perfection
  • Quantify results — numbers are memorable (10,000 queries → 10)
  • Connect to the role — relate stories to what REWE needs
  • Stay positive — even about failures and conflicts

sections.heading: Your Strengths for TRAB
sections.content:
• Enterprise Java — you've maintained complex business systems
  • SQL Performance — you've turned 16-minute processes into 2 seconds
  • Messaging — strong JMS/ActiveMQ production experience
  • Business Rules — complex domain logic implementation
  • Production Debugging — execution plans, traces, logs

sections.heading: Honest Gaps & How to Address
sections.content:
When asked about technologies you haven't used in production:

"I have strong experience with [related technology].
I understand the core concepts of [new technology]
because [specific connection].
I am currently deepening my practical knowledge through
[specific learning activities]."

This applies to: Spring Boot, Kafka, Kubernetes, Angular in production.
